name: playwright-mcp
tagline: ClawHub (156★): Browser automation via Playwright MCP server. Navigate websites, click elements, fill forms, extract data, take screenshots
verdict: Generally solid, with security and quality both good. Troubleshooting only covers install and runtime issues, not in-flow failures like a selector not found or a navigation timeout, so mid-automation breaks aren't well guided. No version tracking, and no stated "NOT for" boundary either.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill language anywhere in description or body.
No version field in frontmatter and no changelog/dated revision notes anywhere in the body.
Official, well-known package but installed unpinned (no version); rubric treats well-known unpinned packages as warning, not fail.
